review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9: Great learning.. Very competitive, PPL says price is high but never felt such situations. Offcourse it's good for only dynamic person and fast moving person. (Suggestions.. Slow moving person should not enter to Havells, rather they will loose there legs and even would not able to walk in future, 😉😅) The quarterly schemes will not make you feel boaring as you always get a different one to mobilize in market. Great team support, not only from your division but also from other divisions The key account management system is great. Coz one concern employee for one dealer makes easy to handle the dealer problem, growth and many more. It's also avoid any complications between the dealer and the company. The monthly meeting and more specifically the weekly meeting were very helpful where we were discussing about the dealers monthly plan , market scenario the collection issues. Although weakly meetings were not for all but for those who were handling the dealers i.e. major dealers. Basically very new comers were not getting the chance. The sallary structure and the increments were standing out from the market. Marketing strategy and state level support system were worked very powerfully in our market. As we were lunching a very new product i.e. Appliances. There are so many things to say.. but keeping it this much..... Overall it's a great experience to work with Havells.

review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9 2:
Crowded with people who were hired just because they are related to someone senior in the company - these people often lack required required skill, burden the skilled ones in team and bring down the efficiency of the team altogether.

No training programs to improve skill level of employees

Mostly, there are no defined job roles- too much time goes into non core activities which basically add nothing to individual learning, and eventually leads to dissatisfaction over time. 

Even after infusing lots of money in ERP, IT environment is abysmal and automation is hard to find in most functions. 

IT team majorly consist of people who have been in the company for decades and held work profiles like manual accounting and warehouse operation type of work. When ERP was implemented, they were made stakeholders of ERP modules. This led to a shoddy ERP implementation and consequently major modules became useless over time.

There are many critical functions which are person dependent, and these people often refrain from knowledge sharing too keep there dominance in the system

Dealers/Distributors are given God-like treatment. But this mostly attributed to market dynamics where Havells operate. It is a competitive market customer loyalty is fickle.

review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9: Work life balance is good. CTO and HRs are very supportive. Very Good Growth (But only at senior level)
review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9 2:
Company is have a dynast culture with all the people at upper level from one single family. This makes policies biased from a perspective of an employee.

 Moreover there is no formal communication about anything, all we get to know is through rumours and unofficial communication. 

The company gives 13 Earned Leaves nd 12 Casual leaves (1/month allowy only) for a year, any other absence apart from this will be reflected back as salary deduction.

Havells have a very unique culture of adjusting the employee bonus in CTC itself. Even the diwali bonus is dedicated from monthly salary and then given back to us on diwali.

(ALL THIS INFORMATION IS GENUINE IN EVERY SENSE FROM MY PERSPECTIVE. BUT PEOPLE WHO HAVE NOT SEEN PERKS AND CULTURE OF OTHER GOOD MULTINATIONALS ARE LIKELY TO ENDORSE HAVELLS)

review-details_detailsLink__NxIv5: The Review is for Sales Profile Only
review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9:
1. Flat Organizational structure - so workwise, there isn't much difference between a manager and a sales executive. The only boss in the branch is the branch head.
2. A good company to start your sales career with. You will understand how money moves in the FMEG sector. Havells is the only electrical company to work in such a way. Others have followed route only post 2013.
3. Every year your salary grows between 15 ~ 30 %, depending on your performance. But 20% is average y-o-y growth for many.
4. Once you have set your channel / market, 7 to 10 days is all you require to work essentially in a month.
5. Stay with the firm between 2 ~ 5 years (max.) to boost up your commercial / sales skills. Then move to the next better bargain.
review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9 2:
1. Travelling and out-station expenses awarded by the company are bad. Despite having become an MNC, the miser baniya culture still exists in certain sections.
2. Starting salaries are still below average. 2 ~ 4 lacs essentially for various colleges and 6 ~ 8 lacs for students from KJ Somaiyah, BIMTEC Noida &  Thapar Univ.
3. The general population in Havells is not very knowledgeable in Electrical Technology. So you will have to study all by yourself to make a difference. This attitude will help you crack companies like Siemens, ABB, Schneider & Eaton Tech.
